channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: First known congressional SpaceX stock buys surface after record IPO
item.description: The SpaceX stock purchases come as Elon Musk’s company deepens its role in federal contracting and remains closely tied to President Donald Trump’s Washington.

item.title: Trump to mark U.S. 250th anniversary with campaign-style rally on National Mall
item.description: Washington's July 4 celebration attendees this year will have to contend with stepped-up ‌security, possible thunderstorms and temperatures that could top 100F.

item.title: The World Cup sends prediction market volumes soaring to record highs
item.description: Trading volumes on Kalshi and Polymarket both hit record highs in June. Rothera, a new prediction market platform, was also able to manage $2 billion in volume.

item.title: Trump Accounts for kids launch July 4: What parents need to know
item.description: Here's a breakdown of how Trump Accounts work, who qualifies for free money and when the funds can be withdrawn.

item.title: 'Just married': Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce tie the knot
item.description: Comedian Adam Sandler officiated the ceremony, said the publicist's statement under the headline "Taylor Swift & Travis Kelce Are Married!"

item.title: Trump hails U.S. exceptionalism before veering into darkly political speech to usher in America's 250th anniversary
item.description: The president's speech capped an Independence Day eve otherwise most notable for a brutal heat wave that gripped much of the eastern part of the country.

item.title: BTIG names an athleisure company, cybersecurity stock and more as its top picks for the second half of 2026
item.description: The firm named 55 large- and small-cap stocks that it thinks are set to perform well in the next six months.

item.title: Erdogan's warm ties with Trump offer Turkey an edge ahead of NATO summit
item.description: Trump's affinity for strongmen leaders made him an admirer of Erdogan, who amassed power in Turkey as its prime minister and in his 13th year as president.

item.title: Pope Leo urges U.S. to welcome immigrants in July 4 appeal
item.description: Leo drew the ire of Donald Trump last year after calling the U.S. president's hardline anti-immigration policies inhumane.

item.title: Iran begins six-day funeral for Ayatollah Khamenei nearly four months after his death
item.description: Iran plans a six-day funeral for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, with ceremonies across Iran and Iraq before burial in Mashhad.

item.title: 5 takeaways from President Donald Trump's interview with CNBC
item.description: The president defended his family's business and said he feels bad that the presidency creates a conflict of interest in any of his children's investments.

item.title: President Donald Trump defends business dealings, his children in exclusive interview with CNBC
item.description: Trump spoke with CNBC as he prepares to celebrate the 250th birthday of the U.S. on July 4 with an event and fireworks on the National Mall in Washington.

item.title: UK and France agree with Oman to ensure safety of its territorial waters
item.description: France said it has deployed mine countermeasures to the Middle East, including two mine-hunting ships.

item.title: Feds seek lower prison term for $100M New Jersey deli fraudster — but some reasons why are hidden
item.description: James Patten is the third person who will be sentenced in the stock manipulation scheme related to Hometown International, a company that owned just one deli.

item.title: From Macron to Modi, governments are rolling out the red carpet for AI giants
item.description: Macron and Modi are courting tech CEOs as France and India seek AI data center investment and cloud infrastructure.

item.title: I've worked with over 5,000 kids: I swear by this No. 1 parenting rule—it's 'surprisingly simple'
item.description: Child development expert Siggie Cohen, who has worked with over 5,000 families, shares one communication mistake she sees parents make every day. Here's why she says it can backfire, when to set clear boundaries and how to use questions more effectively.

item.title: This generator builder catering to AI went public a month ago. Why analysts see big gains
item.description: The rise of AI and data centers is creating a large demand for energy, and many firms on Wall Street believe this company can capitalize on the trend.

item.title: Oman walks a diplomatic tightrope over Strait of Hormuz fees, creating a ‘blind spot’ for markets
item.description: Oman is using strategic ambiguity as talks over possible Strait of Hormuz fees raise legal, diplomatic and oil market concerns.

item.title: Keiko Fujimori declared winner of Peru presidential race
item.description: When Fujimori assumes power on July 28, she will be Peru's 10th president since 2016.

item.title: The hunt for AI's next winners defined the stock market's holiday-shortened week
item.description: Wall Street kicked off the third quarter with mixed results after a blockbuster first half for stocks.

item.title: American Express and Chase move luxury lounge wars beyond the airport
item.description: Credit card companies are increasingly offering access to lounges and perks at festivals and sporting events, often exclusively for premium cardholders.

item.title: Lemonade's $10.5M data breach settlement: See if you qualify and learn how to protect your identity
item.description: A class action suit alleges cybercriminals accessed driver’s license numbers belonging to approximately 190,000 Lemonade customers.

item.title: Ford achieves quality milestone, as CEO targets flawless new vehicle launches
item.description: Ford CEO Jim Farley told CNBC that the automaker has learned from its past quality and recall issues, which have hurt its earnings and stained its reputation.

item.title: Largest U.S. power grid PJM escalates emergency actions to avoid blackouts
item.description: PJM ​said ​it was ​under a federal alert to cut electricity consumption. It serves 67 million people ⁠in the Mid-Atlantic, South and Washington, D.C., area.

item.title: Extreme heat wave threatens U.S. power grids and July 4 travel
item.description: The heat wave threatens to overwhelm U.S. grids and may force some to change their plans on one of the busiest travel weeks of the year.

item.title: Trump’s Freedom 250 draws corporate sponsors with business before his administration
item.description: Companies with federal interests — including Boeing, Lockheed Martin and Oracle — are sponsoring a Trump-aligned effort celebrating America’s 250th birthday.

item.title: Acting DNI Pulte fires dozens of intelligence officials: MS Now
item.description: President Donald Trump's controversial pick to lead the office of the Director of National Intelligence has begun firing intel officials, MS NOW reported.

item.title: Global stock markets close higher as Europe's benchmark hits 52-week high: Live updates
item.description: Europe's Stoxx 600 ended Friday higher, following Asia-Pacific markets, as investors continued rotating out of technology stocks.

item.title: As ACA enrollment falls by millions, Trump administration and policy gurus disagree on why
item.description: Affordable Care Act marketplace enrollment has declined by 3 million people. The Trump administration points to fraud controls. Policy experts point to cost.

item.title: Don't throw away your shot to speak to an AI Alexander Hamilton at Boston's new finance museum
item.description: Nearly a decade after leaving its former home, the Museum of American Finance is opening its new doors to the public.
